_comment: Priority 3.2 Advanced Yoga rules. Evaluated by advancedYogaRuleEvaluator.js using the SAME Rule Engine (services/rules/ruleEngine.js) as rules/yogas.json, extended (not replaced) with new operators: signIn, inOwnSign, inExaltation, inDebilitation, houseDistanceFromIn, conjunctAll, dispositorInHouseGroup, houseLordInHouses, anyOf/allOf/not. Kept in a separate file from yogas.json so Priority 2/3.1's original yoga rule set stays byte-for-byte untouched; this file is additive only. House numbers are Lagna-relative, matching the existing convention (houseIn already checks house-from-Lagna in yogas.json). [Priority 6 / V2.0 addition: added Gajakesari Yoga, Budhaditya Yoga, and basic-support Parivartana Yoga (all 21 mutual-sign-exchange pairs among the 7 classical planets, via the new mutualSignExchange operator). All Priority 3.2 rules above are untouched.] [Correction: Gajakesari Yoga and Budhaditya Yoga were found to already exist in rules/yogas.json (Priority 2, ids gajKesariYoga/budhadityaYoga) and were therefore NOT duplicated here to avoid double-detection; only genuinely new Parivartana Yoga (basic support, all 21 mutual-sign-exchange pairs) was added in Priority 6.]

rules.name: Ruchaka Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha)
rules.detailTemplate: Mars is in its own or exalted sign while occupying a kendra house ({Mars_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Ruchaka Yoga, one of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas.

rules.name: Bhadra Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha)
rules.detailTemplate: Mercury is in its own or exalted sign while occupying a kendra house ({Mercury_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Bhadra Yoga, one of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas.

rules.name: Hamsa Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha)
rules.detailTemplate: Jupiter is in its own or exalted sign while occupying a kendra house ({Jupiter_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Hamsa Yoga, one of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas.

rules.name: Malavya Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha)
rules.detailTemplate: Venus is in its own or exalted sign while occupying a kendra house ({Venus_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Malavya Yoga, one of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas.

rules.name: Shasha Yoga (Panch Mahapurusha)
rules.detailTemplate: Saturn is in its own or exalted sign while occupying a kendra house ({Saturn_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Shasha Yoga, one of the five Panch Mahapurusha Yogas.

rules.name: Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Sun)
rules.detailTemplate: Sun's debilitation in house {Sun_house} is traditionally considered cancelled (Neecha Bhanga) because its sign-dispositor is well placed in a kendra, a combination associated with Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga.

rules.name: Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Moon)
rules.detailTemplate: Moon's debilitation in house {Moon_house} is traditionally considered cancelled (Neecha Bhanga) because its sign-dispositor is well placed in a kendra, a combination associated with Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga.

rules.name: Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Mars)
rules.detailTemplate: Mars's debilitation in house {Mars_house} is traditionally considered cancelled (Neecha Bhanga) because its sign-dispositor is well placed in a kendra, a combination associated with Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga.

rules.name: Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga (Saturn)
rules.detailTemplate: Saturn's debilitation in house {Saturn_house} is traditionally considered cancelled (Neecha Bhanga) because its sign-dispositor is well placed in a kendra, a combination associated with Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga.

rules.name: Viparita Raja Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: A dusthana house lord is placed in a different dusthana house, a configuration traditionally associated with Viparita Raja Yoga (unexpected reversal-of-fortune benefits).

rules.name: Chandra Mangal Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: Moon and Mars conjoin in house {Moon_house}, a combination traditionally associated with Chandra Mangal Yoga (financial drive and enterprise).

rules.name: Lakshmi Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: Venus is in its own or exalted sign while placed in a kendra or trikona house ({Venus_house}), a placement traditionally associated with Lakshmi Yoga (prosperity and grace).

rules.name: Saraswati Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: Jupiter, Venus, and Mercury are all placed in kendra or trikona houses, a configuration traditionally associated with Saraswati Yoga (learning, eloquence, and the arts).

rules.name: Adhi Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: A natural benefic (Mercury, Jupiter, or Venus) occupies the 6th, 7th, or 8th house counted from the Moon, a placement traditionally associated with Adhi Yoga (sustained success and standing).

rules.name: Sunapha Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: A planet other than the Sun occupies the 2nd house from the Moon, a placement traditionally associated with Sunapha Yoga (self-made resourcefulness).

rules.name: Anapha Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: A planet other than the Sun occupies the 12th house from the Moon, a placement traditionally associated with Anapha Yoga (personal comfort and dignity).

rules.name: Durudhara Yoga
rules.detailTemplate: Planets occupy both the 2nd and 12th houses from the Moon, a placement traditionally associated with Durudhara Yoga (wealth and support from others).

rules.name: Parivartana Yoga (Sun-Moon)
rules.detailTemplate: Sun and Moon mutually occupy each other's sign (each is placed in a sign ruled by the other), a configuration traditionally associated with Parivartana Yoga (mutual sign exchange, basic/general support — linking the affairs of the houses each planet rules).

rules.name: Parivartana Yoga (Sun-Mars)
rules.detailTemplate: Sun and Mars mutually occupy each other's sign (each is placed in a sign ruled by the other), a configuration traditionally associated with Parivartana Yoga (mutual sign exchange, basic/general support — linking the affairs of the houses each planet rules).
